New Zealand v Egypt — 22 Jun, 06:30 AM IST
Uruguay v Cape Verde — 22 Jun, 03:30 AM IST
Argentina v Austria — 22 Jun, 10:30 PM IST

Full group standings, scorers and the complete fixture list are on our FIFA World Cup 2026 page, updated through the day.
key_facts: Germany 2-1 Ivory Coast
Ecuador 0-0 Curaçao
Tunisia 0-4 Japan
Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia

stories.headline: Sloppy India lose Manchester thriller to South Africa, in danger of early exit from Women's T20 World Cup 2026
stories.what: India Women suffered their first defeat of the Women's T20 World Cup 2026, going down to South Africa in a thriller in Manchester. Marizanne Kapp's brilliant all-round display proved the difference, leaving India in a precarious position in the tournament standings. The loss raises serious concerns about India's chances of advancing from the group stage, having previously been unbeaten in the tournament.
stories.lens: India now risk an early exit from the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 — a tournament they were among the favourites to win — making their remaining group games must-win affairs.
stories.article:
India Women's unbeaten run at the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 came to an abrupt end in Manchester, where South Africa beat them in a thriller that puts India in serious danger of an early exit from the tournament. The defeat — India's first of the competition — leaves their group-stage progression hanging by a thread, with the margin for error now essentially zero in their remaining matches.

South Africa's Marizanne Kapp was the standout performer, producing the kind of match-winning display that has made her one of the most dangerous players in women's cricket. Kapp's contribution — both with bat and ball — dismantled India's challenge at critical moments, and the Indian side was described as sloppy in their execution, failing to hold their nerve when it mattered most in the tight contest.

The loss is a significant blow for a side that had entered the tournament with genuine title ambitions. India had looked composed in their earlier matches, but South Africa exposed the fragilities that can surface under pressure, particularly in English conditions where the ball can move and the margins are tight.

For India's management and players, the message is stark: there is no more room for error. The next match now takes on the character of a knockout game, and the selectors and coaching staff will need to address the lapses — fielding, decision-making, or batting collapse, whichever proved decisive — before they can afford another performance of this nature. South Africa, meanwhile, will head into their next fixture with considerable confidence.
key_facts: India Women suffer first loss of Women's T20 World Cup 2026
South Africa win a thriller in Manchester
Marizanne Kapp's brilliance was the difference
India now in danger of early group-stage exit

stories.headline: Cricket Ireland breaks silence on security threat to Sooryavanshi's potential India debut
stories.what: Cricket Ireland has publicly addressed reports of a security threat surrounding Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's potential debut for India, which is expected to come in the upcoming series in Ireland. The Irish board broke its silence to respond to concerns that had been swirling around the teenager's first senior international appearance. The situation highlights the heightened scrutiny and logistical complexity that now accompanies Sooryavanshi's every move given his extraordinary rise to fame.
stories.lens: A security threat around a young Indian debutant playing abroad is an unusual and serious development — it speaks to both Sooryavanshi's celebrity status and India's duty-of-care obligations towards players on tour.
stories.article:
Cricket Ireland issued a public statement addressing reported security concerns linked to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's anticipated debut for the Indian senior team, expected during India's tour of Ireland. The Irish board's decision to break its silence suggests the reports had reached a level of seriousness that required an official response, though the specific nature of the threat has not been confirmed in detail.

Sooryavanshi's meteoric rise — from domestic prodigy to IPL sensation and now India A match-winner — has made him one of cricket's most-watched teenagers globally. That level of visibility can attract unwanted attention, and concerns about player safety during overseas tours are taken seriously by both the BCCI and host boards. Cricket Ireland's statement is understood to have sought to reassure all parties that appropriate security arrangements are in place or under review.

The NDTV Sports report framed the development as a question mark over whether Sooryavanshi's debut could proceed as anticipated, though no official postponement or cancellation of the tour has been signalled. Sources suggest both boards remain in communication to ensure the visit goes ahead safely.

The episode underlines the extraordinary pressure that comes with being India's next big cricketing star. Sooryavanshi, still a teenager, is now navigating media scrutiny, selection debates, and security logistics simultaneously — a unique set of challenges that will test the administrative frameworks around him as much as it tests the player himself.
key_facts: Cricket Ireland addressed security concerns around Sooryavanshi's debut
Sooryavanshi's India debut expected during India's tour of Ireland
Nature of the security threat has not been officially detailed

stories.headline: Yashasvi Jaiswal Stars as India Complete Series Sweep
stories.what: Yashasvi Jaiswal led India to a series sweep, delivering a standout performance in the final match to seal a clean series win. The victory underlines India's dominant form with Jaiswal emerging as the decisive batting force across the series. It marks a commanding statement by the Indian side heading into their broader international schedule.
stories.lens: A series sweep confirms India's growing depth and Jaiswal's emergence as a match-winner — a sign of things to come for the national side.
stories.article:
India completed a series sweep with Yashasvi Jaiswal front and centre, the young opener delivering another commanding performance to seal a clean slate for the visiting side. The result caps a dominant run for India across the series, with Jaiswal's contributions at the top of the order proving decisive throughout.

Jaiswal, who has steadily cemented his place as one of India's most reliable batting weapons across formats, once again led from the front in the decisive match. His ability to anchor the innings while also rotating strike and accelerating at will has been the defining feature of India's batting during this series.

The sweep is a significant achievement for the Indian team, reflecting both individual brilliance and collective effort across the squad. For the management and selectors, it provides firm evidence of the team's consistency away from home conditions — always a key benchmark for a side with larger ambitions.

As reported by The New Indian Express, the series result will give the Indian camp considerable confidence ahead of upcoming fixtures. With Jaiswal in this form, India's top order looks increasingly settled, and the series win sends a clear message to future opponents.
key_facts: India complete a series sweep in their recent series
Yashasvi Jaiswal named as the standout performer
Jaiswal's batting was the decisive factor across the series

stories.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes record-breaking 11-ball half-century for India A against Sri Lanka A
stories.what: Teenager Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has set a new record with a stunning 11-ball half-century playing for India A against Sri Lanka A, making it the fastest fifty in the format at this level. The innings underlines the prodigious talent of the Rajasthan Royals youngster who had already announced himself to the world with his exploits in the IPL. The record-breaking knock cements his reputation as one of Indian cricket's most explosive young batting talents.
stories.lens: Sooryavanshi's record 11-ball fifty shows that India's next generation of power-hitters is arriving ahead of schedule — potentially reshaping the country's T20 batting depth for years to come.
stories.article:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has rewritten the record books with an astonishing 11-ball half-century for India A against Sri Lanka A, producing one of the most explosive innings seen at this level of cricket. The teenager, who first captured the nation's imagination with his exploits in the IPL for Rajasthan Royals, showed that his big-stage hitting is no fluke — it is a consistent, repeatable skill that is now turning heads at the representative level as well.

The 11-ball fifty is a record for India A cricket, and it underscores the sheer hitting power Sooryavanshi brings to the top of the batting order. The young right-hander appears to have no fear of reputation or occasion, taking on new-ball bowlers and dispatching them with a clarity and confidence that belies his age. Sri Lanka A's bowlers had no answer as boundaries rained down in an over that will be replayed many times.

Sooryavanshi's rise has been one of the most talked-about storylines in Indian cricket over the past year. Having graduated from domestic age-group cricket into the IPL spotlight, the India A call-up represents the next logical step on what appears to be a rapid and inevitable march toward the senior national side. Selectors and fans will now be watching closely to see whether he can sustain this form across an innings and a series.

For Indian cricket fans, the emergence of a player capable of winning matches in the powerplay with this kind of aggression is genuinely exciting. With the T20 World Cup cycle always on the horizon, Sooryavanshi is firmly knocking on the door.
key_facts: Sooryavanshi's 11-ball fifty is a record at India A level
Innings came against Sri Lanka A
Sooryavanshi previously starred for Rajasthan Royals in IPL
He is among the youngest players to achieve such a milestone

stories.headline: Johnny Lever surprises Zakir Khan on stage at Papa Yaar tour in Mumbai
stories.what: Legendary Bollywood comedian Johnny Lever made a surprise appearance on stage during comedian Zakir Khan's ongoing 'Papa Yaar' tour stop in Mumbai, delighting the audience with an unannounced cameo. Fans in attendance and online erupted with excitement, with many describing the moment as 'The genius with the GOAT', referencing Lever's iconic status in Indian comedy. The clip of the surprise appearance rapidly circulated on social media.
stories.lens: Two generations of Indian comedy — Lever's Bollywood era and Zakir Khan's stand-up generation — sharing a stage is a rare cultural moment that resonated deeply with fans across age groups.
stories.article:
Mumbai audiences at Zakir Khan's 'Papa Yaar' tour got an unexpected treat when Johnny Lever, one of Bollywood's most beloved comedians, walked on stage unannounced to join Khan mid-show. The surprise appearance sent the crowd into raptures and almost immediately went viral, with fans dubbing the moment a meeting of comedy royalty — 'The genius with the GOAT.'

Lever, whose physical comedy and timing made him a household name across three decades of Hindi cinema, and Khan, whose introspective, relatable stand-up style has built him a massive following among younger Indians, represent two distinct but celebrated chapters in the country's comedy tradition. Their on-stage chemistry, even briefly, became a talking point well beyond the venue.

Videos and photographs from the Mumbai show spread quickly across Instagram and X, with fans expressing joy at seeing the veteran comedian embrace the newer wave of Indian stand-up. For many, Lever's appearance was a validation of Khan's standing in the broader entertainment landscape.

The 'Papa Yaar' tour has been one of the notable stand-up events of 2025, and the Mumbai leg — now punctuated by Lever's cameo — is likely to be remembered as its most talked-about night. It also underlines how Indian comedy, once dominated by filmi slapstick, has evolved into a thriving live performance culture that can now pull icons from earlier eras onto its stage.
key_facts: Johnny Lever made a surprise on-stage appearance at Zakir Khan's show
The event was part of Khan's 'Papa Yaar' tour in Mumbai
Fans called the moment 'The genius with the GOAT' on social media

stories.headline: Priyanka Chopra to Star Alongside Angelina Jolie in New Hollywood Film
stories.what: Priyanka Chopra is set to share the screen with Angelina Jolie in an upcoming Hollywood production, marking another major step in her ongoing crossover career. The pairing of two globally recognised stars has generated significant buzz both in India and internationally. Details about the film's plot and release timeline have not yet been disclosed, but the casting itself signals a high-profile project. The announcement underscores Chopra's continued standing as one of the few Indian actors with sustained Hollywood reach.
stories.lens: For Indian audiences, seeing Priyanka Chopra headline a film alongside one of Hollywood's biggest names reinforces that Indian talent is genuinely competing — not just appearing — at the top of global cinema.
stories.article:
Priyanka Chopra is set to co-star with Angelina Jolie in a new Hollywood film, in what is shaping up to be one of the most high-profile cross-cultural castings in recent memory. The pairing brings together two of the entertainment world's most bankable international names, and the announcement has quickly become one of the most talked-about entertainment stories across Indian and global media.

Chopra, who first broke into Hollywood with ABC's Quantico and went on to appear in the Baywatch reboot and the Matrix Resurrections, has steadily built a credible presence in American cinema and television over the past decade. This project, however, represents a qualitative leap — sharing top billing with Jolie, an Oscar winner and one of Hollywood's most enduring A-listers, is a different proposition entirely.

While specific details about the film's genre, director, and release schedule remain under wraps, the casting alone signals that the production carries significant weight. Projects that bring together stars of this calibre typically attract major studio backing and wide international distribution, which would ensure the film reaches audiences well beyond the usual Bollywood diaspora circuit.

For India's entertainment industry, the development carries symbolic as much as commercial significance. Chopra has long been a template for Indian actors eyeing the global stage, and a genuine co-starring role alongside Jolie — rather than a supporting turn — is the kind of milestone that changes what the next generation of Indian film talent believes is possible.
key_facts: Priyanka Chopra to co-star with Angelina Jolie in a Hollywood film
Specific plot, director, and release date not yet disclosed
Chopra's most high-profile Hollywood pairing to date

stories.headline: Sonam Khan reacts to 40-year-old rumour about casting in Tridev song 'Oye Oye'
stories.what: Actor Sonam Khan has publicly addressed a decades-old rumour that she was originally cast in the iconic 1989 film Tridev's song 'Oye Oye', which became a defining hit of that era. Khan has now broken her silence on the claim, offering her version of events around the casting that ultimately featured Sangeeta Bijlani. The story resurfaces periodically in Bollywood nostalgia circles, and her direct response has reignited conversation about the golden age of Hindi film music.
stories.lens: For fans of 1980s and 1990s Bollywood, this rare on-record response to one of Hindi cinema's most persistent casting rumours offers a direct window into how iconic moments were made — and unmade — behind the scenes.
stories.article:
Sonam Khan has finally addressed a rumour that has circulated in Bollywood nostalgia conversations for four decades — that she was originally meant to appear in the song 'Oye Oye' from the 1989 blockbuster Tridev. The track, featuring Sangeeta Bijlani and picturised to become one of Hindi cinema's most recognisable numbers, has long been accompanied by whispers about alternate casting.

Khan's reaction brings a first-person perspective to a story that has previously existed only as industry hearsay. The 'Oye Oye' number, composed by Kalyanji-Anandji and shot with high energy choreography, helped cement Tridev's status as one of the defining commercial films of the late 1980s and launched the song into enduring pop-culture memory.

Whether Khan confirms, denies, or nuances the rumour, the fact that she is engaging with it publicly is itself significant — such candid reflections from actors of that generation about behind-the-scenes decisions are rare. The story touches on how casting choices that seemed routine at the time can take on mythological proportions over the decades.

The response has sparked fresh interest among fans of the era and reignited discussion around Tridev, Sangeeta Bijlani, and the bustling, deal-driven world of 1980s Hindi film production.
key_facts: Rumour dates back to 1989 film Tridev and its song 'Oye Oye'
Song featured Sangeeta Bijlani and became a cult hit
Sonam Khan publicly addressed the casting claim for the first time

stories.headline: 'My qaum are all Indians': Javed Akhtar claps back at troll, reiterates he is an atheist
stories.what: Veteran lyricist and poet Javed Akhtar has hit back at a social media troll, asserting that his community — his 'qaum' — is all Indians, not any religious group. In the same response, Akhtar reiterated that he is an atheist, refusing to be defined by religious identity. The exchange quickly went viral, drawing widespread attention to his unequivocal statement on national identity over sectarian belonging.
stories.lens: In an era of charged identity politics, one of India's most respected cultural voices publicly rejecting religious labelling and claiming all Indians as his people is a moment that resonates far beyond a social media spat.
stories.article:
Javed Akhtar, one of Hindi cinema's most celebrated lyricists and a longtime public intellectual, fired back at a social media troll who had apparently sought to define him by religious identity. In a sharp, measured response, Akhtar declared his 'qaum' — a word meaning community or people — to be all Indians, cutting across religion, caste, and region.

Akhtar, who has consistently described himself as an atheist, used the occasion to restate that position unambiguously. His pushback was characteristically direct: he neither softened his atheism nor accepted any narrower communal identity being projected onto him by his critics online.

The exchange quickly spread across platforms, with many users applauding the clarity and confidence of his reply. Akhtar has long been a vocal critic of religious orthodoxy and communal polarisation, and this latest episode fits a pattern of frank public engagement that has defined his persona well beyond his filmmaking work.

The moment lands at a time when questions of identity, belonging, and national versus religious loyalty are fiercely contested in Indian public life. Akhtar's willingness to engage trolls directly — rather than ignore them — and to state his worldview without hedging continues to make him one of the more distinctive voices in mainstream Indian cultural discourse.
key_facts: Akhtar declared all Indians are his 'qaum', not any religious group
Reiterated he is an atheist in his response to a troll
Exchange went viral on social media

stories.headline: World Music Day: Composer Shankar Mahadevan says Indian musicians are doing wonders on world stage
stories.what: On World Music Day, celebrated annually on June 21, composer and singer Shankar Mahadevan spoke about the growing global footprint of Indian musicians, saying they are "doing wonders on the world stage". Mahadevan, known for his work across Bollywood, Carnatic and fusion genres, reflected on how Indian artists are gaining recognition internationally at an unprecedented pace. His comments spotlight a broader cultural moment for Indian music beyond its traditional domestic boundaries.
stories.lens: As Indian film music, classical traditions, and independent artists increasingly break into global charts and concert halls, Mahadevan's observations point to a genuine cultural export story that affects how India is perceived worldwide.
stories.article:
World Music Day on June 21 brought a thoughtful message from one of India's most accomplished composers: Shankar Mahadevan, speaking to Hindustan Times, declared that Indian musicians are currently "doing wonders on the world stage" — a sentiment that resonates with a string of recent international breakthroughs by Indian artists across genres.

Mahadevan, whose career spans landmark Bollywood soundtracks, Carnatic classical performance and globally recognised fusion projects with the Shankar-Ehsaan-Loy trio, is well placed to comment on the industry's trajectory. He has himself performed on international platforms and collaborated across cultural boundaries, making his observations more than ceremonial.

The growth he points to is visible across several fronts — from A.R. Rahman's continued international touring and collaborations, to younger independent Indian artists charting on global streaming platforms, to Indian classical musicians drawing full houses at European and North American festivals. The digital revolution in music distribution has removed many of the gatekeeping barriers that previously confined Indian music to diaspora audiences.

Mahadevan's World Music Day remarks serve as both a celebration and a marker of where the industry stands. As India's soft power conversation grows louder, music remains one of its most organic and widely felt expressions on the global stage.
key_facts: Shankar Mahadevan spoke on World Music Day (June 21)
Said Indian musicians are 'doing wonders on world stage'
Mahadevan known for Bollywood, Carnatic and fusion work
